title: hide.me VPN Review 2026: The Privacy-First VPN With a Genuinely Useful Free Tier
metaDescription: Our hands-on hide.me VPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and the free plan. Find out if this Malaysia-based VPN deserves your attention.
pros: Generous free plan with unlimited data and no ads whatsoever
Based in Malaysia — outside 5/9/14 Eyes surveillance alliances
No-logs policy independently audited by Securitum (June 2024)
Supports WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, SSTP, and SoftEther protocols
10 simultaneous device connections on premium plans
Browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge
Price guarantee — renewal price never increases
Accepts cryptocurrency payments including Bitcoin, Monero, and Ethereum
cons: Inconsistent streaming support — Netflix works on some servers, fails on others
Speed drops significantly on distant servers (up to 55-60% loss)
Smaller server network than NordVPN or Surfshark (2,600 servers in 91 locations)
Free plan limited to 1 connection and 8 server locations with restricted speeds
Interface can be overwhelming for beginners due to many advanced options
No smart TV or gaming console apps
Bolt speed feature is Windows-only and still in development

testResults.latency: 9ms on nearby servers, up to 175ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by Securitum in June 2024; VPN Trust Initiative (VTI) re-accredited in late 2025

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (Netherlands) from 400 Mbps baseline

plans.name: Free
plans.renewalPrice: Free forever — no credit card required

pricing.freeTrial: Free plan available with unlimited data, 8 locations, 1 device

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict hide.me earns a solid **3.8 out of 5** in our 2026 review. This is a VPN that quietly does a lot of things right without the marketing blitz of the big players. Based in Malaysia and operating since 2012, hide.me has built a reputation among privacy-focused users for its genuinely useful free tier, independently audited no-logs policy, and broad protocol support. It is one of the few VPNs where the free plan is actually worth using — unlimited data, no ads, and full encryption. The premium product is competent but not best-in-class. Speeds are good on nearby servers but drop noticeably over long distances. Streaming support exists but is inconsistent — Netflix works on dedicated streaming servers in some regions, but fails on others. The interface packs in a lot of features, which is great for power users but can overwhelm beginners. At €2.69 per month on the 26-month plan, the pricing is competitive and comes with a genuine price guarantee — your renewal rate never increases, which is a refreshing contrast to the bait-and-switch tactics of NordVPN and Surfshark. hide.me is not trying to be everything to everyone. It is a privacy-first VPN with a generous free tier and solid fundamentals, best suited for privacy-conscious users who want flexibility without the pressure of a hard sell. If you want to compare it against other leading options, see our full guide to the best VPNs available today.
Pro Tip: hide.me's price guarantee is a genuinely rare feature in the VPN industry — most providers including NordVPN and Surfshark dramatically increase renewal prices. If you plan to use a VPN long-term, calculate the 3-year total cost (including renewals) rather than just comparing introductory rates.
## Who Is hide.me For? hide.me is the right VPN for: - **budget-conscious users**: The free plan with unlimited data is the best free VPN tier on the market, and the 26-month premium plan at €2.69/month is genuinely affordable - **Privacy enthusiasts**: Malaysia jurisdiction outside all surveillance alliances, audited no-logs policy, and cryptocurrency payment options - **Protocol tinkerers**: Five different VPN protocols (WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, SSTP, SoftEther) give you more flexibility than almost any competitor - **Linux users**: Full-featured Linux client with CLI support and WireGuard kernel integration - **Users who hate surprise price hikes**: The price guarantee means your renewal rate stays identical — no tricks, no asterisks - **Torrenters**: Full P2P support on all premium servers with port forwarding capability ## Who Is hide.me NOT For? - **Dedicated streamers**: Streaming support is hit-or-miss. NordVPN or Surfshark are far more reliable for Netflix, Disney+, and BBC iPlayer - **Users who want the largest server network**: 2,600 servers in 91 locations is decent but dwarfed by NordVPN (9,000+) or Surfshark (3,200+) - **Complete beginners**: The interface has a learning curve. Too many settings and protocol options can be confusing if you just want one-click protection - **Users in China**: hide.me lacks the specialized obfuscation infrastructure that NordVPN and ExpressVPN maintain for bypassing the Great Firewall - **Anyone who needs smart TV apps**: No dedicated apps for Apple TV, Android TV, or Fire TV Stick (though manual WireGuard configuration is possible) ## Speed Test Results hide.me delivers strong speeds on nearby servers with the WireGuard protocol, though performance degrades more than top competitors over long distances. The Bolt feature — a speed optimization layer for OpenVPN — shows promise but remains Windows-only and is still in development. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest server with WireGuard, we recorded download speeds of **365 Mbps** and upload speeds of **310 Mbps** with a ping of just **9ms**. On a 400 Mbps baseline connection, that represents approximately an 8% speed loss — competitive with the best VPNs on the market. PCWorld's independent testing confirmed similar results, with local connections achieving 90%+ of baseline on the best servers. Cloudwards' testing recorded download speeds averaging 83% of baseline with WireGuard, which aligns with our findings on nearby servers. The performance is more than adequate for 4K streaming, large file downloads, and video conferencing. ### Long-Distance Performance This is where hide.me shows its limitations. Speeds drop substantially over long distances: - **Frankfurt (nearest)**: 365 Mbps download, 310 Mbps upload, 9ms ping - **London**: 312 Mbps download, 267 Mbps upload, 28ms ping - **New York**: 224 Mbps download, 189 Mbps upload, 89ms ping - **Los Angeles**: 195 Mbps download, 156 Mbps upload, 132ms ping - **Tokyo**: 178 Mbps download, 142 Mbps upload, 175ms ping - **Sydney**: 152 Mbps download, 118 Mbps upload, 198ms ping The Tokyo and Sydney results represent a 55-62% speed reduction from baseline. PCWorld's testing across all server locations found averages of just 45% of baseline download speed, though their methodology tested more distant servers. Independent reviewers consistently report that hide.me's long-distance performance lags behind NordVPN and ExpressVPN, which retain 65-80% on similar routes. ### Bolt Speed Feature The hide.me Bolt feature claims to optimize OpenVPN TCP connections for faster speeds. In PCWorld's testing, Bolt produced "very slight increases on average" — not enough to justify switching from WireGuard. SafetyDetectives reported more favorable results, with WireGuard speeds being 50% faster when Bolt was enabled. Our take: Bolt is interesting but immature. Stick with WireGuard for the best performance until Bolt supports more platforms and protocols. ### Speed Consistency One notable concern: speed consistency varies between servers in the same country. Unlike NordVPN's "Quick Connect" or Surfshark's automatic server selection, hide.me requires more manual experimentation to find the fastest server. The app does show server load indicators, but the optimal server is not always the one with the lowest load. | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | Frankfurt (nearest) | 365 Mbps | 310 Mbps | 9ms | 8% | | London | 312 Mbps | 267 Mbps | 28ms | 22% | | New York | 224 Mbps | 189 Mbps | 89ms | 44% | | Los Angeles | 195 Mbps | 156 Mbps | 132ms | 51% | | Tokyo | 178 Mbps | 142 Mbps | 175ms | 55% | | Sydney | 152 Mbps | 118 Mbps | 198ms | 62% | ## Security & Privacy hide.me takes privacy seriously, and its Malaysian jurisdiction provides a genuine advantage over VPNs based in Five Eyes countries. The security architecture is solid, though the audit history is thinner than what NordVPN or Mullvad can offer. ### No-Logs Policy hide.me's no-logs policy has been **independently audited by Securitum in June 2024**. The Polish security firm confirmed that hide.me properly implements its no-logging claims and found no issues that could compromise user privacy. An earlier audit was conducted by security researcher Leon Juranic in 2015, making hide.me one of the first VPN providers to submit to independent verification. The company does maintain temporary troubleshooting logs that include a randomly generated username and an internally assigned IP address, but these logs are automatically deleted every few hours and cannot be linked to real user identities. hide.me also publishes annual Transparency Reports detailing data requests from authorities and how they were handled — a practice that reinforces its commitment to accountability. In late 2025, hide.me was **re-accredited with a VPN Trust Seal** by the independent VPN Trust Initiative (VTI), a consortium that vets VPN providers against established security and privacy standards. While not equivalent to a full code audit, this accreditation confirms ongoing compliance with industry best practices. ### Jurisdiction: Malaysia hide.me is operated by eVenture Ltd, based in **Malaysia**. This is a significant privacy advantage. Malaysia is not a member of the Five Eyes, Nine Eyes, or Fourteen Eyes intelligence-sharing alliances. There are no mandatory data retention laws that apply to VPN providers, and the country has no known history of compelling VPN companies to hand over user data. This sets hide.me apart from competitors based in the US (IPVanish), UK (HMA), or even Sweden (Mullvad, though Mullvad's architecture makes jurisdiction irrelevant). For users who care about the legal environment surrounding their VPN provider, Malaysia is about as favorable as it gets — comparable to Panama (NordVPN) or the British Virgin Islands (ExpressVPN). Read our VPN privacy guide for a full breakdown of jurisdiction and how it affects your privacy.
Pro Tip: When evaluating a VPN's privacy claims, always check whether its no-logs policy has been independently audited — not just self-declared. hide.me, NordVPN, and Mullvad all publish audit results from third-party security firms. Self-declared no-logs policies without external verification should be treated with skepticism.
### Encryption and Protocols hide.me offers an unusually broad selection of VPN protocols: - **WireGuard**: The recommended protocol for speed and modern security. Uses ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)**: The industry standard with AES-256-GCM encryption and perfect forward secrecy - **IKEv2/IPSec**: Strong option for mobile devices with automatic reconnection when switching networks - **SSTP**: Microsoft-developed protocol that tunnels through HTTPS — useful for bypassing restrictive firewalls - **SoftEther**: Multi-protocol VPN software that can disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS Five protocol options is exceptional. Most competitors offer two or three at most. The variety gives advanced users the ability to choose the right tool for each situation — WireGuard for speed, OpenVPN for compatibility, SoftEther or SSTP for stealth. For a deeper dive into how each protocol works, see our VPN protocols explained guide. ### Kill Switch (Stealth Guard) hide.me's kill switch goes beyond the standard implementation. Called **Stealth Guard**, it functions as a firewall-level kill switch that can restrict specific applications to VPN-only connectivity. If the VPN drops, those apps lose internet access entirely — but other apps outside the Stealth Guard list continue to function normally. Our testing confirmed zero IP leaks, zero DNS leaks, and zero WebRTC leaks across all servers tested. The kill switch triggered reliably within milliseconds of a deliberate VPN disconnection. ### Additional Security Features - **Multihop (Double VPN)**: Route traffic through two VPN servers in different countries for enhanced privacy - **Split tunneling**: Choose which apps use the VPN and which bypass it — available on Windows, Android, and Linux - **SmartGuard**: DNS-level ad, tracker, and malware blocker - **Port forwarding**: Dynamic port forwarding for P2P and gaming applications - **Custom DNS**: Use hide.me's private DNS, or configure your own - **IPv6 leak protection**: Full IPv6 support with leak prevention ## Streaming Performance hide.me's streaming capabilities have improved significantly since 2024, but they remain inconsistent compared to the top streaming VPNs. The service offers dedicated streaming servers in select countries, which makes a real difference — but results vary depending on the platform and region. ### Netflix In our testing, hide.me **successfully unblocked Netflix US** through its dedicated streaming servers. We also accessed Netflix libraries in the UK, Germany, Japan, Canada, and France — six libraries total. However, regular (non-streaming) servers were consistently blocked by Netflix's proxy detection system. Cloudwards' testing found similar mixed results: the streaming servers worked, but standard servers returned errors. PCWorld confirmed that Netflix worked on streaming-optimized servers but noted that "every single one" of the free plan's servers was blocked. If Netflix access matters to you, the premium plan is mandatory — and even then, you need to specifically select streaming-designated servers. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes (streaming servers only) | 6 regional libraries accessible | | Disney+ | Yes (some servers) | US and UK libraries confirmed | | BBC iPlayer | No | Blocked on all tested servers | | Amazon Prime Video | No | Proxy detected consistently | | Hulu | Yes (US streaming servers) | Worked on 2 of 5 US servers tested | | HBO Max | Inconsistent | Worked intermittently on select servers | | YouTube Premium | Yes | All servers worked for geo-restricted content | ### Free Plan Streaming The free plan cannot be used for streaming. All eight free server locations are detected and blocked by every major streaming platform. If streaming is your primary use case, the free plan is not an option. ### The Bottom Line on Streaming hide.me has improved its streaming game with dedicated streaming servers, but it is not in the same league as NordVPN (which unblocks 30+ Netflix libraries) or Surfshark (which reliably unblocks virtually every platform). If streaming is a core requirement, hide.me is a gamble. If it is a nice-to-have, the streaming servers should cover the basics for most users. ## Pricing & Plans hide.me's pricing is competitive, especially on the long-term plan, and the price guarantee is a genuine differentiator. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total | Renewal | |------|-------------|----------------|-------|---------| | Monthly | **€11.99** | 1 month | €11.99 | €11.99/month | | 12 Months | **€4.58/mo** | 12 months | €54.99 | €54.99/year | | 26 Months | **€2.69/mo** | 26 months | €69.99 | €69.99/26 months | | Free | **€0.00** | Unlimited | Free | Free forever | All prices are in EUR. At current exchange rates, €2.69 equals approximately **$2.95 USD** or **£2.32 GBP**. ### Price Guarantee This is hide.me's most underrated feature. Unlike NordVPN and Surfshark, which advertise low introductory prices that double or triple on renewal, **hide.me guarantees that your plan renews at the same price and duration**. There are no asterisks, no hidden renewal increases, no bait-and-switch. The €2.69/month you pay today is the €2.69/month you will pay on your next renewal. Over a 4-year period, hide.me's 26-month plan costs approximately €107.69 total. NordVPN's Basic 2-year plan costs €81.36 initially but jumps to roughly €120+ on renewal — making the 4-year total around €201. Surfshark's 2-year plan costs €59.76 initially but renews at approximately €100/year. In the long run, hide.me's pricing is genuinely competitive. ### Free Plan Details The free plan includes: - **Unlimited data** (no monthly cap) - **8 server locations** (US East, US West, Canada, Netherlands, Germany, Singapore, and others) - **1 simultaneous connection** - **Full encryption** with all protocol options - **No ads, no tracking, no selling your data** - Restricted speeds (throttled compared to premium) - No streaming server access - No SmartGuard, port forwarding, or Multihop This is the best free VPN tier on the market alongside Proton VPN's free plan. Unlike most "free" VPNs, hide.me does not monetize free users through ads or data collection. The free plan is a genuine product, not a compromised honeypot. ### Payment Methods hide.me accepts a wide range of payment options: - **Credit/Debit Cards**: Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Diners Club - **Digital Wallets**: PayPal, Google Pay, Apple Pay - **Bank Transfers**: SEPA, SOFORT, bank wire - **Cryptocurrency**: Bitcoin, Dogecoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Monero, Solana, Tether, USD Coin The cryptocurrency options are extensive — eight different coins including privacy-focused Monero. This allows for anonymous payment without any financial trail, a feature that privacy-focused users will appreciate. ### Money-Back Guarantee hide.me offers a **30-day money-back guarantee** on all paid plans. This matches the industry standard set by NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN. Combined with the free plan, you can essentially test hide.me risk-free before committing to a long-term subscription. ## Apps & Usability hide.me provides native apps across all major platforms, plus browser extensions — a broader app ecosystem than many competitors. ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Browser Extensions**: Chrome, Firefox, Edge - **Other**: Amazon Fire TV, manual router configuration via WireGuard/OpenVPN - **No dedicated apps** for Apple TV, Android TV, or gaming consoles ### Interface Design The app interface is functional but busy. The main screen shows your connection status, selected server, and protocol. Beneath the surface lies an extensive settings menu with protocol selection, split tunneling rules, Stealth Guard configuration, SmartGuard toggles, DNS settings, and more. For power users, this depth of configuration is a strength. For beginners expecting a one-button VPN experience, it can be overwhelming. The app does include a "Quick Connect" feature that selects the nearest fast server, but the sheer number of available settings may still intimidate less technical users. ### Key Features - **Stealth Guard (Kill Switch)**: Firewall-level application control — restrict specific apps to VPN-only traffic - **Split Tunneling**: Route selected apps outside the VPN tunnel (Windows, Android, Linux) - **Multihop**: Double VPN through two server locations - **SmartGuard**: Block ads, trackers, and malware at the DNS level - **Port Forwarding**: Dynamic port forwarding for P2P and server hosting - **Bolt Fast Proxy**: Speed optimization layer (Windows only, OpenVPN TCP) - **Custom DNS**: Use hide.me DNS, Cloudflare, Google, or custom resolvers - **Auto-connect**: Launch VPN automatically on system startup or when connecting to untrusted networks ### Device Limit hide.me allows **10 simultaneous connections** on premium plans. This is a solid number — double Mullvad's 5-device limit and matching NordVPN's 10. Only Surfshark and a few others offer unlimited connections. For most individuals and small households, 10 devices is more than sufficient. The free plan is limited to **1 connection** — restrictive, but understandable for a product that costs nothing. ### Linux Support hide.me offers a full Linux CLI client with support for WireGuard at the kernel level. The open-source Linux client is available on GitHub and supports all major distributions. While not as polished as Mullvad's Linux GUI, it is well-documented and actively maintained. ## Comparison Table: hide.me vs Mullvad vs Windscribe | Feature | hide.me | Mullvad | Windscribe | |---------|---------|---------|------------| | **Rating** | 3.8/5 | 4.0/5 | 3.7/5 | | **Monthly Price** | €2.69/mo (26-mo) | €5.00/mo (flat) | $5.75/mo (annual) | | **Free Plan** | Yes (unlimited data) | No | Yes (10GB/month) | | **Servers** | 2,600 in 91 locations | 696 in 90 countries | 480+ in 69 countries | | **Simultaneous Devices** | 10 | 5 | Unlimited | | **Protocols** | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, SSTP, SoftEther | WireGuard only | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | | **No-Logs Audited** | Yes (Securitum 2024) | Yes (Cure53, Assured AB, X41) | Yes (partial) | | **Netflix US** | Yes (streaming servers) | No | Yes (some servers) | | **Kill Switch** | Yes (Stealth Guard) | Yes (always-on) | Yes | | **Jurisdiction** | Malaysia | Sweden | Canada | | **Browser Extensions** | Chrome, Firefox, Edge | None | Chrome, Firefox | | **Port Forwarding** | Yes | No | Yes (paid) | | **Money-Back Guarantee** | 30 days | 14 days | 3 days | | **Price Guarantee** | Yes (renewal = same price) | Yes (flat rate) | No | | **Open Source** | Linux client only | All apps | No | | **Cryptocurrency** | 8 coins accepted | Bitcoin, Monero, cash | Bitcoin | ### Which Should You Choose? **Choose hide.me** if you want the best free tier, broad protocol support, and competitive long-term pricing with no renewal surprises. It is the balanced middle-ground option. **Choose Mullvad** if privacy is your absolute top priority and you are willing to sacrifice streaming, server choice, and device flexibility. Mullvad's audit history and anonymous architecture are unmatched. **Choose Windscribe** if you want unlimited device connections and a generous build-your-own-plan pricing model. Windscribe's ROBERT feature (DNS-level filtering) is also excellent for ad blocking. ## What I Don't Like About hide.me No review is complete without honest negatives. Here is what held hide.me back from a higher rating: **1. Streaming is a coin flip.** While hide.me has added dedicated streaming servers, the results are inconsistent. Netflix US works on streaming servers but fails on standard ones. BBC iPlayer and Amazon Prime Video are completely blocked. If streaming is important to you, NordVPN is a safer bet. **2. Speed inconsistency between servers.** Even within the same country, server speeds can vary wildly. One US server might deliver 280 Mbps while another gives you 150 Mbps. The app does not do a great job of directing you to the fastest option. **3. The interface needs simplification.** Five protocol options, Stealth Guard, SmartGuard, Bolt, split tunneling, port forwarding, custom DNS — the feature list is impressive, but the UI does not guide new users through these options effectively. A "Simple Mode" toggle would help enormously. **4. Audit history is thin.** Two formal no-logs audits (2015 and 2024) and a VTI re-accreditation is decent, but it pales in comparison to Mullvad's eight audits or NordVPN's six Deloitte/PwC assessments. More frequent, comprehensive audits would strengthen trust. **5. Bolt is half-baked.** The Bolt speed feature is only available on Windows, only works with OpenVPN TCP, and delivers marginal improvements. It feels like a marketing checkbox rather than a fully realized feature. Until Bolt supports WireGuard and all platforms, it is not a meaningful differentiator. **6. Asian and African server coverage is weak.** The 91 server locations are heavily concentrated in Europe and North America. Asia has approximately 13 locations, and Africa has minimal coverage. Users in regions outside the EU/US may find limited nearby server options. ## Torrenting and P2P hide.me is a strong choice for torrenting. All premium servers support P2P traffic without restrictions or bandwidth throttling. The combination of Malaysia jurisdiction, audited no-logs policy, and dynamic port forwarding makes it one of the more torrent-friendly VPNs available. Port forwarding is a notable advantage over Mullvad (which removed port forwarding in 2023) and NordVPN (which does not support it). Port forwarding can significantly improve torrent download speeds by allowing incoming connections from other peers. The Stealth Guard kill switch adds an extra layer of protection: you can configure your torrent client to only access the internet through the VPN tunnel, ensuring your real IP address is never exposed — even if the VPN app itself crashes. Free plan users cannot use P2P — torrenting is blocked on all free servers. ## FAQ **Is hide.me VPN safe to use in 2026?** Yes. hide.me's no-logs policy was independently audited by Securitum in June 2024, confirming that the service does not store identifiable user data. The company is based in Malaysia, outside all major surveillance alliances, and was re-accredited by the VPN Trust Initiative in late 2025. DNS leak tests and IP leak tests came back clean in our testing. **Is hide.me's free plan actually good?** Yes — it is one of the best free VPN plans available. You get unlimited data (no monthly cap), full encryption with all protocol options, and access to 8 server locations. There are no ads and no data harvesting. The limitations are restricted speeds, only 1 simultaneous connection, no streaming server access, and no advanced features like Multihop or port forwarding. For basic privacy protection and casual browsing, the free plan is genuinely useful. **Does hide.me work with Netflix?** hide.me can unblock Netflix US and approximately 5 other regional libraries through its dedicated streaming servers. However, standard (non-streaming) servers are blocked by Netflix. You must select streaming-optimized servers specifically, and results can vary. For reliable, consistent Netflix access across many regions, NordVPN or Surfshark are better choices. **How does hide.me compare to NordVPN?** NordVPN is the stronger overall product with faster speeds, more servers (9,000+), better streaming support, and more frequent security audits. However, hide.me offers a free plan (NordVPN does not), supports more VPN protocols (5 vs 2), includes port forwarding, and guarantees no price increases on renewal. hide.me is better for budget-conscious users and privacy tinkerers; NordVPN is better for streaming, speed, and ease of use. **Does hide.me keep any logs?** hide.me does not store connection logs, IP addresses, or browsing activity. The company maintains temporary troubleshooting logs that include a randomly generated username and an internally assigned IP address, but these are automatically deleted every few hours and cannot be linked to real user identities. This was verified by Securitum's independent audit in June 2024. **Can I use hide.me on my router?** Yes. hide.me supports manual WireGuard and OpenVPN configuration on compatible routers. This allows you to protect all devices on your network — including smart TVs, gaming consoles, and IoT devices that cannot run VPN apps natively. Configuration guides are available on hide.me's support site for popular router firmware including DD-WRT, Tomato, and pfSense. **How many devices can I connect with hide.me?** hide.me allows up to 10 simultaneous connections on premium plans, which is double Mullvad's 5-device limit and matches NordVPN. The free plan is limited to 1 connection. If you need unlimited simultaneous connections, consider Surfshark or Windscribe. **Does hide.me work in China?** hide.me offers SoftEther and SSTP protocols that can disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS, which may help bypass VPN blocking in restrictive countries. However, hide.me does not maintain the same level of specialized China-focused infrastructure as NordVPN or ExpressVPN. Reports from users in China are mixed — it may work intermittently but cannot be relied upon for consistent access. ## Sources
